title: Michel Gondry Reunites With Jim Carrey in ‘Kidding’ | The Big Picture (Ep. 83)
format: Sean Fennessey talks to Michel Gondry about moving to TV, reuniting with Jim Carrey, and why he shows the seams of how things are made.

films.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
films.note: His last film with Jim Carrey; he calls Carrey's role there a turning point that the new melancholy of Kidding builds on.

films.title: Be Kind Rewind
films.note: Named as a signature Gondrian work while discussing his handmade, show-the-process aesthetic.

films.title: Dave Chappelle's Block Party
films.note: The concert documentary cited as proof he chases magic across formats.

films.title: Is the Man Who Is Tall Happy?
films.note: His animated Noam Chomsky conversation, another format he's stretched into.

films.title: Won't You Be My Neighbor?
films.note: The Mr. Rogers documentary; Gondry saw it only after arriving in the U.S. and told Carrey not to watch Rogers tapes so Mr. Pickles wouldn't become mimicry.

films.title: BlacKkKlansman
films.note: His pick for the last great thing he's seen; he thanks Spike Lee for showing D.W. Griffith as the racist he was.

films.title: Gone with the Wind
films.note: The film's opening shot that BlacKkKlansman fixes on and that Sean and Gondry dwell on.

Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind

Is the Man Who Is Tall Happy?.title: Is the Man Who Is Tall Happy?
